title: QA: lsp.rs child.wait() after shutdown has no timeout (deadlocks on misbehaving server)
description:
## Severity
Minor
## Dimension
concurrency
## Location
src/tools/lsp.rs:226-229
## Violation
After issuing `shutdown` + `exit` notifications, `run_lsp_request` calls `child.wait()` with no timeout. A misbehaving language server (or one that received malformed input earlier and is wedged) will block this call forever — and because `execute_lsp` is called synchronously from the tool dispatch loop, the entire agent stalls.

Quoted (lsp.rs:226-229):
```
    // Shutdown
    send_lsp_message(&mut stdin, "shutdown", 3, json!(null))?;
    send_lsp_notification(&mut stdin, "exit", json!(null))?;
    let _ = child.wait();
```

The `let _` also discards any exit status; we cannot tell if the server crashed.
## Mandated refactor
1. Spawn a watchdog thread that calls `child.kill()` after a bounded shutdown deadline (~2s).
2. Or use `wait_timeout` crate / `nix::sys::wait::waitpid(WNOHANG)` polling with a deadline.
3. Surface the wait result so a server crash during shutdown is logged.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
